title: Easy Tiling

content.problemStatement:
We wish to tile a grid of size Nx2 with rectangles (dominoes) of 2x1 (in either orientation).

Write a program that takes input N and outputs the number of different ways to tile the grid. Output the ans mod 10^9 + 7
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N denoting the length of the board (grid size N×2).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of ways to tile an N×2 board with 2×1 dominoes, modulo 1000000007.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N=1 there is only one vertical domino. For N=2 there are two ways: two vertical dominoes or two horizontal dominoes stacked.
The numbers follow the Fibonacci sequence shifted by one: f(3)=3, f(4)=5, f(5)=8.

solution.approach: Tiling a 2×N board with 2×1 dominoes follows the recurrence f(N) = f(N-1) + f(N-2) with base f(0)=1, f(1)=1. This is exactly the Fibonacci sequence shifted by one: f(N) = Fib(N+1). To answer each test case efficiently we compute Fib(N+1) modulo 1e9+7 using the fast‑doubling method, which returns (F(k), F(k+1)) in O(log k) time. Since T ≤ 10 and N ≤ 1000, this is easily fast enough, and the same routine works for any larger constraints if needed.
solution.timeComplexity: O(log N) per test case, O(T log N) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(log N) recursion stack depth, O(1) extra memory.
